result.stdout:
Usage:
	-c, --config[optional]... Path to the config file to use. Defaults to Leprechaun.config. 

	-w, --watch[optional]... If set, source files are watched for changes and automatically rebuilt. 

	-h, --help[optional]... Prints this message 

	-n, --noexit[optional]... If set, the program will not exit after completion until a key is pressed. Mostly for debugging purposes. 

	-g, --grumpycat[optional]... Disables the ASCII art splash screen. You hate fun. 

	-r, --rainbow[optional]... Use legacy console app. Required for input providers that don't support .NET Core (like Rainbow) 

	-p, --plugins[optional]... Path to the folder where plugins will be located. ./Leprechaun 

	-d, --debug[optional]... Prints out full stack traces for exceptions

commands.payload:
Usage:
	-c, --config[optional]... Path to the config file to use. Defaults to Leprechaun.config. 

	-w, --watch[optional]... If set, source files are watched for changes and automatically rebuilt. 

	-h, --help[optional]... Prints this message 

	-n, --noexit[optional]... If set, the program will not exit after completion until a key is pressed. Mostly for debugging purposes. 

	-g, --grumpycat[optional]... Disables the ASCII art splash screen. You hate fun. 

	-r, --rainbow[optional]... Use legacy console app. Required for input providers that don't support .NET Core (like Rainbow) 

	-p, --plugins[optional]... Path to the folder where plugins will be located. ./Leprechaun 

	-d, --debug[optional]... Prints out full stack traces for exceptions
